payload.title: 116th Street Bus & Pedestrian Priority Project – Updated Proposal
payload.description: Presentation to Manhattan Community Board 10's Transportation, Historic Preservation & Landmarks Committee regarding the 116th Street Bus & Pedestrian Priority Project updated proposal.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: Updated proposal presented to CB10's Transportation, Historic Preservation & Landmarks Committee in June 2025. Includes offset bus lanes, pedestrian spaces, concrete pedestrian islands, left turn lanes, mid-block crossings, bus queue jump signal, and parking changes along 116th Street from Manhattan Avenue to Pleasant Avenue.
CB10 study area: 116th St., Manhattan Av., and Morningside Av. between 114th St. and 113th St. Total 1.1 miles. Critical crosstown service corridor.
Operates along 116th Street in the CB10 study area. Weekday congestion causes 785 hours of delay to M116 passengers daily.

evidence_refs.source_quote: 785 hours of delay to M116 passengers daily

payload.description: Repurposed slip lane for pedestrians and potential programming at Morningside Avenue at W. 113th Street. 8,600+ ft2 of programmable plaza space. Hardened by granite blocks and vertical elements.
Three proposed concrete pedestrian islands, protected by bollards, with potential trees. Calms left-turning drivers, adds refuge space to intersection.
Nine proposed left turn lanes. Maintains traffic flow and improves safety. Adds organization and safety for all road users.
One proposed concrete pedestrian curb extension at 116th St. and Adam Clayton Powell Jr. Dr. Shortens crossing distance with expanded sidewalk space. Reduces crossing distance by 12%.

tool_args.label: Signal Phase Change: Three-Phase to Two-Phase

payload.claim_text: Simpler traffic signal cycle will save time for drivers and bus riders on Morningside Avenue and Manhattan Avenue. Changes from three-phase signal (Manhattan Ave NB/SB 45s, 113th St 23s, Morningside Ave SB/Manhattan Ave NB 22s) to two-phase signal (Manhattan Ave NB/SB 67s, 113th St 23s) per 90s light cycle, adding +22s of green time for NB/SB travel.
payload.text: Simpler traffic signal cycle will save time for drivers and bus riders
